q: In pure water, very little dissociation takes place.
The number of hydroxide ions that water will receive determines the pH of the solution.
Having both proton-accepting and proton-donating properties, water is called an amphiprotic molecule.
In reactions with bases like $NH_{3}$, water acts as a base to facilitate the ionisation process.
The ionic product of water ($K_{w}$) is the product of the molar concentrations of $H^{+}$ and $OH^{-}$ ions at a particular temperature.
Since the process of autoiionisation is exothermic, the concentrations of hydrogen ions increase with temperature.
At $25^{\circ}C$, the value of the ionic product of water ($K_{w}$) is $1.0 \times 10^{-14} mol^{2} dm^{-6}$.
The pH scale ranges from 1 to 14 to measure the acidity or alkalinity of a solution.
In a neutral aqueous solution at $25^{\circ}C$, the concentrations of $H^{+}$ and $OH^{-}$ ions are both equal to $1.0 \times 10^{-7} mol dm^{-3}$.
At $25^{\circ}C$, the sum of pH and pOH for any aqueous solution is always equal to 7.
pOH is defined as the negative logarithm to base 10 of the molar concentration of the hydroxide ions.
For acids, the larger the pH value, the higher the concentration of $H^{+}$ ions and the stronger the acid is.
The pH and pOH scales express the acidity and alkalinity of dilute aqueous solutions.
Neutral aqueous solutions have a pH value of 0 according to the pH scale diagram.
Alkalis have pH values greater than 7, and the larger the pH value, the stronger the alkali is.
The equilibrium concentration of undissociated water differs appreciably from the original concentration because water ionises to a large extent.
The potency of the hydrogen (pH) scale was proposed in 1909 by a Danish biochemist named Soren P. Sorenson.
At $100^{\circ}C$, the value for $K_{w}$ is roughly 50 times smaller than the value at $25^{\circ}C$.
In pure water, the concentration of $H^{+}$ ion is always equal to the concentration of $OH^{-}$ ion.
pOH is calculated as the positive logarithm to base 10 of the molar concentration of hydroxide ions.
